reasoning: Multiple authoritative climate bodies, including Copernicus (reporting on Nov 6, 2025), confirm that 2025 is consistently tracking as the second or third hottest year on record, trailing behind 2024 and 2023. For the first half of 2025, global temperatures were 0.08°C cooler than 2024's record pace. Recent months (September and October 2025) both registered as only the third-warmest for their respective months, significantly trailing the records set in those months during 2023/2024. Given that we have 10 months of data and 2025 is lagging by roughly a tenth of a degree cumulatively, it is mathematically virtually impossible for November and December to single-handedly flip the annual average to a new all-time high above 2024, especially under prevailing La Niña conditions. Therefore, the chance of 2025 being the hottest year on record is exceptionally low.
